title: Creating Diffusion Tensor Images (DTI)
author: Laurel Dieckhaus
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6x2hfqe

steps.title: Open a new terminal
steps.instruction: Open a new terminal.

steps.title: Create a directory to place data
steps.instruction: Create a directory to place data. (If not already created.) mkdir mkdir ~/Data/Projects/ Create directory. 0-58-generic #64-Ubuntu SMP

steps.title: Go to the directory that you plan to use
steps.instruction: Go to the directory that you plan to use. cd cd Go to a specified directory. 0-58-generic #64-Ubuntu SMP

steps.title: Copy directory where your raw data...
steps.instruction: Copy directory where your raw data files that will be acquistioned, reside in. You will need the following file types: Bvals Bvecs data.img data.hdr* - ( may not need this in all cases - used for FOV origin)

steps.title: Find where the files you plan to copy are located
steps.instruction: Find where the files you plan to copy are located. Make sure to use the original root i.e /home/... as you will get an error if you start from another location. ls ls To list files in a specific directory. 0-58-generic #64-Ubuntu SMP

steps.title: Copy the specific files that you need
steps.instruction: Copy the specific files that you need. Alternatively, you can use . to specify the current directory you are in. cp cp Copy file or entire directory to a specified location. 0-58-generic #64-Ubuntu SMP

steps.title: Perform conversion between different file types
steps.instruction: Perform conversion between different file types. mrconvert mrconvert MRtrix3

steps.title: Import data from nifti format to TORTOISE list file format
steps.instruction: Import data from nifti format to TORTOISE list file format. A new directory will be created and the folder will be labeled ____proc . Example: ImportNIFTI ImportNIFTI TORTOISE

steps.title: Go to the new directory that...
steps.instruction: Go to the new directory that was created from the previous step.

steps.title: Correct motion
steps.instruction: Correct motion. Use DIFFPREP command. ( This is a command from TOROISE ). Note: This step is time & core consuming. Also, this step removes Gibbs ringing and noise DIFFPREP DIFFPREP Corrects motion. TORTOISE

steps.title: Open one of the populated files using ITKsnap
steps.instruction: Open one of the populated files using ITKsnap.

steps.title: Within ITKsnap, select Active Contour (aka...
steps.instruction: Within ITKsnap, select Active Contour (aka Snake) Segmentation Mode from the toolbar.

steps.title: A new side window will appear
steps.instruction: A new side window will appear. Select Segment 3D .

steps.title: Toggle the thresholds (lower & upper)...
steps.instruction: Toggle the thresholds (lower & upper) so that the blue mask covers the region you want repelled. ( Regions that are empty space or not the brain of interest should have this mask ). Note: Try not to change the defaults.

steps.title: When the thresholds have been finalized, select Next
steps.instruction: When the thresholds have been finalized, select Next .

steps.title: Set the cursor in the relative...
steps.instruction: Set the cursor in the relative center of your object of interest. Select Add bubble at cursor . A red circle(seed/bubble) will be placed at the location. Note: You may need to repeat this if you have multiple objects of interest.

steps.title: When the bubbles have been set...
steps.instruction: When the bubbles have been set and you have no more to add, select Next .

steps.title: Press the play buton to have...
steps.instruction: Press the play buton to have the seed grow within the mask that was set-up in a previous step. Note: Red should propagate and fill the entire region(s) of interest. This may require viewing the images from the different planes to confirm.

steps.title: Once the region(s) of interest are completely filled, select Finish
steps.instruction: Once the region(s) of interest are completely filled, select Finish.

steps.title: Press Update in the lower left...
steps.instruction: Press Update in the lower left corner of the ITKsnap interface to view the new 3D segmentation image.

steps.title: Select Segmentation and Save As from the drop-down menu
steps.instruction: Select Segmentation and Save As from the drop-down menu. You will be prompted to save your file as a __.nii.gz file type. It is recommended you name your file __mask to clarify segmentation has already been performed.

steps.title: Confirm what directory you are currently in
steps.instruction: Confirm what directory you are currently in. pwd pwd 0-58-generic #64-Ubuntu SMP

steps.title: Close out of ITKsnap
steps.instruction: Close out of ITKsnap.

steps.title: Estimate diffusion tensor and Bo amplitude
steps.instruction: Estimate diffusion tensor and Bo amplitude. EstimateTensorNLLS EstimateTensorNLLS -i ___DTI_DMC.list -m ___mask.nii Generates diffusion tensor and B0 amplitude. TORTOISE This command will generate 2 files: ___N1_DT.nii (diffusion tensor) ___DT_AM.nii (B0 amplitude)

steps.title: In an open terminal, unzip the ___
steps.instruction: In an open terminal, unzip the ___.nii.gz file that was segmented in the previous step. gunzip gunzip ___.nii.gz Unzip file(s) of interest.

steps.title: Compute all tensor maps on the ___N1_DTI
steps.instruction: Compute all tensor maps on the ___N1_DTI.nii output file from the previous step. ComputeAllTensorMaps.bash ComputeAllTensorMaps.bash ___N1_DT.nii Computer all tensor maps. TORTOISE

steps.title: Open the ___DEC
steps.instruction: Open the ___DEC.nii output file in ITKsnap to view the primary eigenvector map.

steps.title: From the Layer Inspector window, select...
steps.instruction: From the Layer Inspector window, select the Display mode drop-down menu and select RGB Display .

steps.title: The final result will show a...
steps.instruction: The final result will show a colorized image of your region of interest that allows a visual guide that estimates location, orientation, and anisotropy of the brain's white matter tracts based on the diffusion of water molecules.

steps.title: Press Update in the lower left...
steps.instruction: Press Update in the lower left corner of the ITKsnap interface to view the image in the new display mode.
